annotation_target.structure: Middle section atmospheric antecedent — flowing triplets, impressionistic shifts
teaching_goals: the middle section has a more flowing, continuous quality than the lyrical sections

style_tips: continuous flow — no accents on beat 1

teaching_notes.note: The harmonic colors shift impressionistically — don't analyze, listen.
technique: follow the harmonic color with slight dynamic shading

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 31–34 of Clair de Lune (Suite bergamasque, III), view the piano roll, analyze the phrase, play in a loop, and predict the continuation.

session.content: Show me measures 31–34 of Clair de Lune — what does the middle section feel like?
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 31–34.

Measures 31–34 are in the middle section — a more active, rippling passage with flowing triplets. The character shifts from the lyrical melody to a more fluid, impressionistic texture. The harmonics shift impressionistically. Let me loop mm. 31–34.
